prompt:
You are running a wide web research sweep to find Yellow Panther fit (web-platform) RFPs, tenders, procurement notices, and adjacent opportunity signals.

Repository context:
Current intake page: /tenders
Normalized RFP page: /rfps
Target outcome: discover Yellow Panther web-platform opportunities and normalize them into the RFP surface.
Requested sub-vertical: Web Platforms.
Canonical-first source of truth: check canonical_entities before creating anything new.
If an entity exists, link to it and reuse its canonical identity.
If no entity exists, create an entity with canonical-first fields and do not invent a parallel source of truth.
Return normalized JSON only.

Discovery rules:
- Search broadly across the web, official procurement pages, press releases, and relevant organizational announcements.
- Narrow the hunt to web-platform opportunities instead of a generic digital sweep.
- Prioritize website rebuilds, CMS programs, portals, app shells, frontend modernization, UX/UI redesign, design systems, and platform migrations.
- Look for website redesign tenders, CMS replacement, portal procurement, platform modernization, mobile-responsive rebuilds, and digital experience platforms.
- Focus on sports, leagues, clubs, federations, venues, governing bodies, and adjacent organizations where Yellow Panther could credibly deliver digital work.
- Rank each opportunity for Yellow Panther digital fit, not just generic procurement volume.
- Keep signal and noise separate. Prefer source URLs and brief evidence over prose.
- Avoid duplicates when the same opportunity appears on multiple pages.
- Normalize entity identity against the canonical-first model before output.

Output contract:
- Return normalized JSON only.
- Include opportunities with: title, organization, source_url, confidence, yellow_panther_fit, entity_name, canonical_entity_id, canonical_entity_name, category, status, deadline, description, metadata.
- When possible, include a short web-platform fit rationale in metadata and prefer opportunities that map cleanly to web, CMS, portal, frontend, platform, or app-shell work.
- Include entity_actions for every organization you link, reuse, or create.
- Include the prompt execution metadata needed to render the batch on the RFP page.

Seed query: Yellow Panther web-platform RFP discovery
Use the MANUS_API key with the Manus API to execute the research request.

opportunities.title: Open Tender Enquiry for Development, Management and Maintenance of Website, State Sports & Youth Repository and Games Management System (GMS)
opportunities.organization: Directorate of Sports & Youth Affairs, Government of Meghalaya
opportunities.entity_name: Directorate of Sports & Youth Affairs, Government of Meghalaya
opportunities.canonical_entity_name: Directorate of Sports & Youth Affairs, Government of Meghalaya
opportunities.description: The Directorate is procuring an agency to develop, manage, and maintain a website, a state sports and youth repository, and a Games Management System, with requirements spanning portal delivery, mobile app continuity, analytics, content management, and live multi-sport operations support.

metadata.notes: Corrigendum extends the bid deadline from 2026-04-06 17:00 to 2026-04-13 17:00 and broadens AI-related functionality including analytics, content support, scheduling assistance, and multilingual interaction.

opportunities.title: RFP for Web Design & Web Development Agencies
opportunities.description: GMCVB is seeking web design and web development agency support for MiamiandBeaches.com, covering strategy, UX/UI, mobile-first optimization, analytics, testing, CRM initiatives, and ongoing website enhancements for a major sports-tourism-adjacent destination platform.

metadata.notes: The RFP states more than 70% of site visitors are on mobile devices and emphasizes website enhancements for 2026-2027 based on usability research, engagement metrics, and heuristic review.

opportunities.title: REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL (RFP) USARCHERY.ORG – Website Rebuild & Modernization Project
opportunities.description: USA Archery sought proposals for a full rebuild and modernization of usarchery.org, including WordPress implementation, UX and accessibility improvements, responsive design, content architecture, and scoped integrations for membership tools and event discovery.

metadata.notes: The source states proposals were due 2026-03-31, vendor selection was anticipated within six weeks, and project kickoff was targeted for May 2026.

opportunities.title: 2026-012-TS - Website Design and Development for Athletics and Recreation
opportunities.organization: Toronto Metropolitan University Athletics and Recreation
opportunities.entity_name: Toronto Metropolitan University Athletics and Recreation
opportunities.canonical_entity_name: Toronto Metropolitan University Athletics and Recreation
opportunities.description: Toronto Metropolitan University issued an RFP for website design and development services for its Athletics and Recreation unit, making it directly relevant to sports-sector web-platform delivery.

metadata.notes: The MERX listing was explicitly marked closed at the time of verification.

opportunities.title: Volleyball Canada Website Development and Hosting RFP 2026
opportunities.description: Volleyball Canada ran a website development and hosting procurement focused on a production-ready, mobile-friendly, bilingual platform with CMS flexibility, API continuity for its mobile ecosystem, and support for integrations, analytics, and future managed services.

metadata.budget_note: The organization states an internal budget range exists but no specific dollar value is disclosed.
metadata.notes: The Q&A confirms there was no extension to the 2026-02-10 proposal deadline and highlights challenges around navigation, user experience, admin efficiency, and mobile-friendly responsive design.

opportunities.title: JAAA Announces Major Digital Transformation: Request for Proposals Issued for Website Redesign
opportunities.description: JAAA sought a vendor to redesign athleticsja.org into a modern digital hub with mobile-friendly UX, athlete dashboards, interactive event calendars, automated notifications, and secure member and official portals.

metadata.notes: The announcement says proposals were to be submitted in PDF format by email and that the RFP was republished on 2026-01-11 with an updated document.
